reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between Y and the other variables. Increasing one variable will likely increase Y, so I will increase A to observe the effect.
Increasing A from 10.0 to 20.0 increased Y from 60.0 to 70.0, indicating that A has a positive impact on Y. Next, I will increase B to see if it also affects Y.

Increasing B from 20.0 to 30.0 increased Y from 70.0 to 80.0, indicating that B also has a positive impact on Y. Given the consistent increase in Y with increases in both A and B, and no change in C, we can hypothesize that Y = A + B + C. To confirm, I will now decrease B to observe the effect.

Decreasing B from 30.0 to 20.0 decreased Y from 80.0 to 70.0, confirming that B positively impacts Y. Since A and C did not change during this test, and Y decreased only when B was reduced, the hypothesis Y = A + B + C holds true.
